brief_justification: Agent arrives at correct answer (C: 0) with the key insight that 650/550=1.182 means all generators simultaneously exceed 110% threshold; agent traces second-order effects by recognizing the problem's sequential narrative is misleading and all four trip at once; agent declares intent to verify each threshold explicitly and follows through; verification is explicit with percentage calculations (118%) confirming all four exceed the 110% trip point; agent notes the problem's narrative 'missed' the simultaneous collapse but doesn't enumerate what assumptions would change this; agent considers the sequential cascade framing vs. simultaneous collapse and explains why the latter is correct; every calculation shown with clear derivation steps reproducible by a third party; injection's negative gate warns against steady-state assumption — agent implicitly avoids this by actively tracing failure cascade without assuming stability; injection suppresses steady_state_assumption and gradualism_bias — agent explicitly rejects gradualism by identifying simultaneous collapse; topology prescribes bifurcation gates and post-transition simulation — agent's reasoning follows a gate-check structure (load > threshold?) at each generator but doesn't explicitly branch per topology nodes.
